exemplar.title: Finding the volume and surface area of a vase to determine how much it would cost to 3D print and coat the vase with epoxy resin.

detailedExemplarData.title: Finding the volume and surface area of a vase to determine how much it would cost to 3D print and coat the vase with epoxy resin.

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear framing of the investigation with a precise title and aim
Wellâorganized structure with numbered sections, headings and referenced figures/tables
Logical development of ideas in a coherent sequence from introduction through evaluation
weaknesses: Occasional irrelevant narrative (e.g. workspace description) reduces conciseness
Minor jumps when omitting working (switch to Python/GDC) disrupt full logical flow
Some repetitive content (full Lagrange expansions shown twice) that could be trimmed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates a high level of coherence and logical development, with clear sectioning and internal referencing. Organization is well above adequate, though small misplacements and verbosity prevent a top mark. Conciseness is mostly achieved but marred by some extraneous detail, placing it firmly in band 3 (out of 4).

strengths: Consistent and mostly precise use of mathematical notation (integrals, subscripts, limits)
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, labelled diagrams, GeoGebra and 3-D screenshots)
Clear logical structure in derivations, with wellâsignposted arguments and defined equations
weaknesses: Typographical slips (missing superscripts, g'I instead of gâ²) affect clarity
Inconsistent subscript notation at times leading to potential confusion
Key justifications (e.g. midpoint averaging in IVT) are stated without full explanation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language is used consistently and accurately through most of the report, and multiple forms of representation are effectively integrated. The overall communication is clear and logical (band 3), despite occasional typos and minor lapses in standardization of notation.

strengths: Genuine independent thinking in reconstructing a lost CAD file via manual interpolation
Strong personal approach evidenced by own measurements, photographs and cost decisions
Good creativity in dividing the vase into regions and verifying methods with software
weaknesses: Mathematical methods applied are largely conventional rather than innovative
Personal narrative, while clear, does not push beyond standard modelling strategies
Some sections rely on textbook techniques without novel extension
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows clear evidence of personal engagement through original problem context and initiative. However, the choice of methods remains within standard practice, aligning with band 2 (out of 3) for this criterion.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on measurement uncertainty and modeling assumptions
Thoughtful evaluation of strengths and limitations (precision, wastage, estimation)
Relevant suggestions for future improvements (water displacement, resin trials)
weaknesses: Reflection is descriptive rather than deeply critical or theoretical
Limited exploration of alternative mathematical approaches or verification methods
Could enhance discussion by connecting to broader mathematical learning outcomes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate provides consistent, meaningful reflection and clear evaluation of outcomes, but lacks the depth and critical insight required for band 3. This positions the work solidly in band 2 (out of 3).

strengths: Appropriate and fully courseâlevel calculus methods (interpolation, integration, unit conversion) applied correctly
Explicit modeling assumptions and sophisticated use of the Mean Value Theorem for surface area
Thorough validation steps, including manual derivations checked against GeoGebra
weaknesses: Occasional skipped algebraic steps when moving to GDC reduces transparency
Minor transcription errors in limits and rounding artifacts introduce small inaccuracies
A conceptual mix-up between Riemann sums for volume versus surface area needs refinement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant, commensurate with HL level and applied effectively, demonstrating good understanding and mostly correct results. Minor errors prevent a higher band, so it appropriately receives band 4 (out of 6).

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are generally well sign-posted; derivations lead into applications; limits, domains and substitutions are explained.  Despite some algebra being skipped with the comment âusing my GDCâ, the communication remains clear and logical overall, meeting the descriptors for band 3.

Derivatives, integrals and numerical results are largely correct; a few minor transcription errors (e.g. derivative mis-typed, upper limit 7.8 instead of 7.48) are noticed but do not materially affect final conclusions.  Understanding is good and mostly accurate, supporting band 4 rather than 5 or 6.

The student employs algebraic expressions, tables of data, labelled diagrams, photographs, GeoGebra graphs and a 3-D revolution screenshot.  These representations complement each other and are clearly linked to the text, constituting âeffective useâ of multiple forms.

The exploration follows a clear sequenceâintroduction, background derivations, modelling, calculation, cost analysis, conclusion and evaluation.  Each part builds sensibly on the previous one and the reader can follow the development of ideas without getting lost.  Minor jumps exist (e.g. sudden omission of working when switching to Python/GDC) but these do not seriously disrupt logic, therefore the work is judged coherent rather than merely âsomewhatâ coherent.

Manual derivations, verification with software and extension to economic costing show creativity and initiative above the minimal level, yet the mathematical techniques themselves remain conventional, so the middle band fits best.

The evaluation section analyses strengths (segmented model, digital plotting) and limitations (ruler precision, over-/underestimation, filament wastage).  Analysis is thoughtful but remains descriptive; it does not probe alternative mathematical strategies or verify numerical results experimentally, hence band 2.

Outcomes are evaluated in terms of cost and feasibility; future suggestions (water displacement, resin trials) are offered.  This shows a clear evaluation with some implications, matching the criteria for band 2.

The student reflects on measurement uncertainty, modelling assumptions (symmetry, perfect surface) and practical material loss.  Reflection is woven into conclusion and evaluation sections rather than isolated, providing âmeaningfulâ though not deeply critical reflection.

Mathematical symbols (â«, Ï, derivatives, subscripts) are used appropriately and consistently through most of the report.  Occasional typographical slips (g'I instead of gâ², missing ^ on squares) are present but do not hamper understanding, so usage meets the âmostly consistentâ description.

The work is clearly personal: the studentâs own photograph, measurements, cost figures and decision-making (e.g.ejecting point G) demonstrate a personal approach beyond a textbook exercise, but the approach is not especially innovative.

Although most content is relevant, the write-up is lengthy and occasionally repetitive (full Lagrange expansions shown twice, lengthy quotations of formulas already available, repeated images).  Some extraneous narrative about camera lenses and personal workspace does not assist the mathematics.  Hence the exploration is âmostly relevantâ but not yet âconciseâ.

All mathematics (polynomial interpolation, integration for volume/surface area, cost conversions) is directly relevant to the aim.  The calculus applied is appropriate for AA HL but not pushed to sophisticated optimisation or error analysis, so ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ (band 4) best fits.

Choosing to reconstruct a lost CAD file through mathematics, splitting the profile into three regions and insisting on performing a full manual Lagrange interpolation shows genuine independent thinking, though the methods themselves are standard.

Headings, numbered sections and tables make the overall structure transparent; introduction, aim, method, calculation, conclusion and evaluation are all present.  Figures and tables are referenced, so internal organisation is clear.  A few sections are misplaced (derivation appears before a statement of data collection) but overall the organisation is well above âadequateâ.

Polynomial interpolation, numerical integration and unit conversions are solidly at HL level and applied correctly in context.  The work is fully commensurate with course level but not exceptionally advanced or novel, justifying band 4.

annotations.comment: There is a typographical error in â(x a around the x-axisâ that obscures the meaning; ensure all variables and intervals are correctly presented for clarity.
The stepâbyâstep derivation of the volume of revolution is well structured, using precise notation for limits and integrals, which supports clear mathematical communication.

The explorationâs title and aim are clearly stated, framing the investigationâs objective of determining volume and surface area for cost estimation. This clarity facilitates logical development and reader engagement.
The line âCandidate code: xxxxxxâ is administrative and irrelevant to the mathematical discussion; its removal would enhance conciseness and maintain focus on content.
The studentâs personal narrative about 3D-printing origins effectively demonstrates genuine motivation and personal engagement beyond standard textbook tasks.
The introductory section includes some extraneous narrative about workspace and lens perspective that does not directly support the mathematical aim; consider trimming to improve conciseness.
The assumption that the vase is an ideal solid of revolution is explicitly stated, demonstrating an appropriate modeling simplification that is clearly justified.
The derivation transitions to using the GDC for simplification, skipping intermediate algebraic steps; consider including one manual example to reinforce understanding and verify accuracy.
The surface area derivation correctly uses frustums and the Mean Value Theorem, showcasing a sophisticated application of calculus concepts appropriate for HL level.

The description of Riemann sums leading to surface area contains a conceptual mix-up between volume and area under a curve; refine this explanation to accurately reflect the lateral surface derivation.
Using clear diagrams (Figure 4) to illustrate frustum approximations effectively supports multiple representations, aiding visual understanding of the derivation.
The notation for intermediate points x_a, x_b and x_f is inconsistent in places, leading to potential confusion; standardize subscripts and explain each clearly.
The use of the Mean Value Theorem to replace average rates of change with derivatives is an insightful approach, showing strong mathematical understanding.
When introducing the Intermediate Value Theorem, the statement f(x_b) = Â½[f(x_f)+f(x_{f-1})] lacks justification; clarify why the midpoint yields the average of the y-values.
Defining the final integral for surface area with proper limits and notation in Equation 1 on page 8 demonstrates rigorous mathematical communication and clarity.
The description of the GeoGebra plotting process includes unnecessary detail about pixel-perfect zooming; summarizing key steps would maintain relevance and conciseness.
Selecting two reference points (J and P) and calculating radius demonstrates effective multiple representation and precise data handling.
In Table 2, point labels and coordinate ordering are clear, but adding units to the table headings would improve presentation and avoid ambiguity.
The first attempt to fit a single polynomial in Figure 5 shows critical thinking in testing methods, illustrating independent exploration of modeling strategies.
The rationale for dividing the vase into three regions is well justified, reflecting thoughtful problem solving and meaningful personal engagement.
When presenting Lagrangeâs formula, the long expanded form occupies excessive space and interrupts flow; consider summarizing the general form and referencing a detailed appendix if needed.
The manual Lagrange interpolation for Part C correctly applies the formula through five points, showing significant independent thinking and algebraic skill.
The final simplified polynomial exhibits minor rounding artifacts; clarify rounding conventions and include error bounds to reinforce accuracy.
Verification of manually derived functions against GeoGebra output confirms accuracy and demonstrates thorough understanding and validation of results.
When determining boundary intervals for each function, the description is verbose; consider presenting boundaries in a concise table to improve organization.
The calculation of A_{top} uses correct substitution into the surface area integral, and the final numerical evaluation is clearly presented.
In the derivative for g'_1(x), there is a typo â2.09604^{2}â which appears incorrect; verify the exponent notation and recalculate to avoid miscommunication.
The step to calculate A_{middle} for the constant section is efficient and clearly simplifies the integral, reflecting good grasp of integral properties.
The final volume calculation for the bottom section incorrectly uses an upper limit of 7.8 instead of 7.48, introducing a discrepancy; ensure consistency of bounds.
Cost calculations effectively convert between units of area, volume, and mass, applying real-world data to the mathematical results with clear reasoning.
When calculating epoxy cost, the text describes unit conversions in multiple steps; consolidating these into a single, streamlined calculation would improve conciseness.
The conclusion revisits the aim and summarizes findings coherently, but it could be enhanced by reflecting on the mathematical learning journey and potential extensions.
The evaluation section thoughtfully mentions measurement uncertainty and modeling assumptions, showing meaningful reflection on limitations and real-world applicability.
